Examples.Title: Get All Model Details
Examples.Description: Get information of all models in an account, irrespective of the type of model (OCR model or Image Classification model).
Examples.Setup: This example assumes `using Nanonets;` is in scope and `username` / `password` contain the required credentials.

Examples.Title: Get Model Details by ID
Examples.Description: Retrieve a specific model's details given its ID.

Examples.Title: Prediction for Image File
Examples.Description: Given an image file, extract data as per the label(s) specified in the model.

Examples.Title: Prediction for Image URLs
Examples.Description:
Given image URL(s), extract data as per the label(s) specified in the model.
Multiple image URLs per request are allowed.

Examples.Title: Train Model
Examples.Description:
Train a model after uploading files to the model.
The same end point is to be used to re-train a model after uploading more files to improve the model.

Examples.Title: Upload Training Images by File
Examples.Description:
Upload a training image to the specified model using a locally stored file. On successful upload, model information along with the total number of images per category will be received.
**NOTE**: Batch file uploads are not supported by this end point.

Examples.Title: Upload Training Images by URLs
Examples.Description:
Upload training images to a model using image URLs.
Multiple images can be uploaded in the same request by adding more URLs in the `urls` array.
On successful upload, model information along with the total number of images per category will be received.

Examples.Title: Get Model Details by ID
Examples.Description: Retrieve a specific model's details given its ID.

Examples.Title: Prediction for Image File
Examples.Description: Given an image file, extract data as per the label(s) specified in the model.

Examples.Title: Async Prediction for Image File
Examples.Description:
Given an image file, extract data as per the label(s) specified in the model, <u>asynchronously</u>.
This end point will send back a response as soon as the file has been uploaded, with a file ID. To retrieve the extracted data from the uploaded file, please use this file ID and hit the [Get Prediction File by ID](#operation/ocr/predict/id) end point.

Examples.Title: Get All Prediction Files
Examples.Description: Retrieve the files that have been processed by the prediction end points.

Examples.Title: Get Prediction File by ID
Examples.Description: Retrieve the prediction results of a particular file by its ID.

Examples.Title: Prediction for Image URLs
Examples.Description:
Given image URL(s), extract data as per the label(s) specified in the model.
Multiple image URLs per request are allowed.

Examples.Title: Async Prediction for Image URLs
Examples.Description:
Given image URL(s), extract data as per the label(s) specified in the model, <u>asynchronously</u>.
This end point will send back a response as soon as the file has been uploaded, with a file ID. To retrieve the extracted data from the uploaded file, please use this file ID and hit the [Get Prediction File by ID](#operation/ocr/predict/id) end point.

Examples.Title: Train Model
Examples.Description:
Train a model after uploading files to the model.
The same end point is to be used to re-train a model after uploading more files to improve the model.

Examples.Title: Upload Training Images by File
Examples.Description:
Upload a training image to the specified model using a locally stored file. On successful upload, model information along with the total number of images per category will be received.

**NOTE**: Batch file uploads are not supported by this end point.

Examples.Title: Upload Training Images by URLs
Examples.Description:
Upload training images to a model using image URLs.
Multiple images can be uploaded in the same request by adding more URLs in the `urls` array.
On successful upload, model information along with the total number of images per category will be received.
